title: Crispy Fermented Ukrainian Potato Salad
description: A bright, tangy Ukrainian potato salad featuring fermented cabbage, crispy fried potato cubes, and a sweetginger duck sauce infused with bhuna spices for a satisfying crunch. Serve chilled for a light, refreshing lunch dish.
instructions: Peel and cut potatoes into 1‑cm cubes.
Place potatoes in a pot, cover with cold water, and bring to a boil. Cook 7–8 minutes until just tender. Drain and let cool.
While the potatoes are boiling, finely slice the red onion and chop the dill and parsley.
Heat the sunflower oil in a non‑stick skillet over medium heat. Add the cooled potato cubes and fry 3–4 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden and crispy. Remove from the pan and set aside.
In a large bowl, combine the fermented sauerkraut, sliced onion, dill, and parsley.
In a small bowl, whisk together the duck sauce, apple cider vinegar, cumin, coriander, mustard seeds, and smoked paprika until smooth.
Pour the dressing over the sauerkraut mixture and toss to coat evenly.
Fold in the crispy potato cubes and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Let the salad chill in the fridge for 15 minutes before serving.
